QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_78

question.text: The daily settlement price for futures contracts is computed based on:

answer.text: The last 30 minutes volume-weighted average price (VWAP) of the futures contract across exchanges
The closing price of the futures contract on the exchange where it was traded
The opening price of the next trading day
The simple average of the day's high and low prices of the futures contract

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Daily MTM settlement uses the last 30-minute VWAP of the futures contract across exchanges. If the contract was not traded during the last 30 minutes, a theoretical price is computed as F = S × e^(rt). Final settlement uses the last 30-minute VWAP of the UNDERLYING (not the futures). **The Trap:** Daily settlement uses futures VWAP. Final settlement (at expiry) uses UNDERLYING VWAP. Different bases for different settlement types. **Cross-Exam Context:** Settlement price computation is tested in Series VIII and I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Daily MTM = last 30-min futures VWAP. Final = last 30-min underlying VWAP. Across exchanges.
